proven.claim: PROVEN: when childForFieldName("function") returns null, optional chaining
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 334
; PROVEN: when childForFieldName("function") returns null, optional chaining
; prevents the member_expression check; the call_expression case returns null.

(declare-const fn_is_null Int)  ; 1 = childForFieldName returned null, 0 = returned a node
(declare-const result Int)       ; 0 = null, 1 = "string", 2 = "number"

; Precondition: we are in the call_expression switch branch
; Code transition: fn?.type === "member_expression" evaluates to undefined === ... → false
;                  if-block is skipped → fall through → return null
(assert (= fn_is_null 1))
(assert (=> (= fn_is_null 1) (= result 0)))

; Negation: result is not null (attempt to falsify the property)
(assert (not (= result 0)))
(check-sat)
; expect: unsat — null fn forces result=0, contradicting result≠0

proven.claim: PROVEN: only member_expression function types are recognized.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 334
; PROVEN: only member_expression function types are recognized.
; Identifier functions (parseInt, String, etc.) yield null — the if-block is guarded
; by fn?.type === "member_expression" and no other branch exists.

; fn_type: 0 = null, 1 = "member_expression", 2 = "identifier" or other
(declare-const fn_type Int)
(declare-const result Int)  ; 0 = null, 1 = "string", 2 = "number"

; Precondition: fn is non-null but not a member_expression (e.g., parseInt(x))
(assert (= fn_type 2))

; Code transition: if (fn?.type === "member_expression") fails
;                  → fall through to: return null
(assert (=> (not (= fn_type 1)) (= result 0)))

; Negation: result is not null
(assert (not (= result 0)))
(check-sat)
; expect: unsat — non-member-expression fn always yields null, contradicting result≠0
